channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump deporting criminal aliens to Guantanamo Bay: Meet the hardened terrorists they'll join
item.description: Meet the terrorist prisoners at Guantánamo Bay that the illegal criminal migrants will join following President Donald Trump's executive order to use the infamous prison to house migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he wants to negotiate about Ukraine. It's not clear if Putin really does
item.description: Nearly three years after President Vladimir Putin invaded Ukraine, his troops are making steady progress on the battlefield. Kyiv is grappling with shortages of men and weapons. And the new U.S. president could soon halt Ukraine's massive supply of military aid.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Crews rush to recover commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice before expected snow and wind
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Just hours after finding 10 people dead in western Alaska from one of the deadliest plane crashes in the state in 25 years, authorities raced to recover their remains and the wreckage of the small commuter plane from unstable sea ice before expected high winds and snow.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on leave and giving them 30-day deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, ordering a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump is signing up local law officers to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: For years, the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office in suburban Indianapolis has wanted to partner with federal immigration authorities to identify and detain immigrants who are in the U.S. illegally and facing charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Generations later, a remedy to destroying Black neighborhoods is fulfilled in Michigan
item.description: HAMTRAMCK, Mich. (AP) — Leslie Knox was a young girl in the 1960s when her Detroit-area city was accused of destroying neighborhoods to get rid of Black residents.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A hidden gem of whitewater rafting faces an uncertain future after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ERWIN, Tenn. (AP) — After 24 years of guiding whitewater trips on the Nolichucky River Gorge for other companies, Patrick Mannion finally received a permit last year to operate his own outfitter business.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KRON4

item.title: Judge in Boston to consider latest bid to block Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston said on Friday he would take under advisement a request from 18 state attorneys general to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration orders federal agencies to provide lists of underperforming employees
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has ordered all federal departments and agencies to provide lists of employees who are underperforming, as it seeks to shrink the workforce and awaits a court ruling related to its deferred resignation offers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows Frontier passenger tackled after allegedly banging window mid-flight
item.description: An unruly passenger on a Frontier Airlines flight was tackled by fellow passengers Tuesday after they say he began punching an aircraft window.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louisiana woman arrested following death of Kansas City reporter covering Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Louisiana woman has been arrested after she was allegedly caught using the reporter's credit cards in the New Orleans area.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ appeals block of birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: It's the first Justice Department appeal of a lawsuit challenging major Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Question on Jeffrey Epstein ties prompts reported foul-mouthed response from new UK ambassador to US
item.description: Newly minted U.K. Ambassador to Washington Peter Mandelson had a terse response to a reporter's question about his prior ties to trafficker Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Parents of teacher found dead with 20 stab wounds offer theory on how she died after 'suicide' ruling reversed
item.description: The City of Philadelphia will reexamine the case of Ellen Greenberg, and her father believes investigators should be looking at strangulation as a possible cause of death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reid G. Miller, longtime AP international correspondent and editor, dies at 90
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Reid G. Miller, who traveled the planet as an intrepid international correspondent for The Associated Press and developed a reputation as a supportive editor and unswervingly loyal boss during the toughest of breaking-news moments, has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's Gaza plan shocks the world but finds support in Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to seek U.S. ownership of the Gaza Strip and move out its population infuriated the Arab world. It stunned American allies and other global powers and even flummoxed members of Trump’s own party. The reaction in Israel was starkly different.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Real estate agent commissions mostly unchanged since new NAR rules
item.description: New real estate commission rules took effect in August, but so far, not much has changed, according to a Redfin report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: 12 states to sue over DOGE access to payment systems containing personal data
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law," a statement from the Democratic attorneys general reads.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Crayola brings back 8 retired colors: Here's what they are
item.description: Crayola is going back to the drawing board with its latest collection, bringing back a special assortment of retired colors for the first time in its 122-year history.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: Couple sues Walgreens, Optum Rx, saying son died after $500 price rise for asthma meds
item.description: A Wisconsin couple is suing Walgreens and a pharmacy benefits management company, alleging that their son died because he couldn’t afford a sudden $500 spike in his asthma medication.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Storm threatens significant ice accumulations in several mid-Atlantic states, forecasters say
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — A winter storm approaching several mid-Atlantic states is threatening significant ice accumulations that could cause widespread power outages and disrupt travel, forecasters warned Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, ABC 4

item.title: In bid for Davos-like gathering, Semafor plans to bring at least 200 CEOs to Washington conference
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The media company Semafor is planning a Washington economic conference for global CEOs this April that it hopes will rival the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, for influence.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service flip-flops on Hong Kong-China packages, lifting a ban imposed a day earlier
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is reversing course a day after placing a ban on all inbound packages from China and Hong Kong. The post office had announced Tuesday that it would no longer accept parcels from the China and Hong Kong after the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 take place in cities across the country
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DeSantis goes to battle with Florida Republicans in trying to get closer to Trump
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — With an open presidential primary coming in 2028, Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is launching an offensive to win back supporters of the Republican leader whom he dared to challenge in the last election.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A timeline of last week's air disaster in Washington, the deadliest in the US since 2001
item.description: It has been nearly a week since a commercial jetliner and an Army helicopter collided in midair near Washington’s Ronald Reagan National Airport, killing 67 people in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he wants US to take charge of Gaza Strip at press conference with Netanyahu
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip and redevelop it after Palestinians are resettled elsewhere. He made the remarks in a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Treasury tells Congress that DOGE has ‘Read Only’ access to payment systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Treasury Department official wrote a letter Tuesday to federal lawmakers saying that a tech executive working with Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency will have “read-only access” to the government's payment system.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN & JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4
